name: run
context: Create and start Docker containers with port mapping, volume mounts, environment variables, and resource limits
info: This sub-skill covers docker run and its many flags for creating and starting containers. docker run combines docker create and docker start in one command. Key configuration includes port mapping (-p host:container), volume mounts (-v or --mount), environment variables (-e or --env-file), network selection (--network), resource limits (--memory, --cpus), restart policies (--restart), user override (--user), working directory (--workdir), and entrypoint/command override. Containers run in the foreground by default; use -d for detached mode. Use --rm for ephemeral containers that are removed on exit. Use --name for human-readable identification. Interactive containers use -it for terminal allocation. This sub-skill covers the run command in depth; see [[docker/containers/exec]] for interacting with running containers and [[docker/containers/logs]] for viewing output.
triggers: start a container from an image
body:
# Running Docker Containers

`docker run` creates a new container from an image and starts it. It is the most commonly used Docker command, combining `docker create` and `docker start` in one step.

## Basic Usage

```bash
# Run and attach to output
docker run nginx:1.25-alpine

# Run in detached mode (background)
docker run -d nginx:1.25-alpine

# Run with a name
docker run -d --name webserver nginx:1.25-alpine

# Run and remove on exit (ephemeral)
docker run --rm alpine echo 'hello world'

# Run interactively with a terminal
docker run -it ubuntu:22.04 /bin/bash

# Run with custom command
docker run -d --name api myapp:latest node server.js --port 3000
```

## Port Mapping

Expose container ports to the host network:

```bash
# Map host port 8080 to container port 80
docker run -d -p 8080:80 nginx

# Map multiple ports
docker run -d -p 8080:80 -p 8443:443 nginx

# Bind to localhost only (not exposed externally)
docker run -d -p 127.0.0.1:8080:80 nginx

# Random host port assignment
docker run -d -p 80 nginx
docker port <container_id>  # check assigned port

# UDP port mapping
docker run -d -p 53:53/udp dns-server

# Map all EXPOSE'd ports to random host ports
docker run -d -P nginx
```

See [[docker/networking]] for network drivers and DNS resolution.

## Volume Mounts

Persist data and share files between host and container:

```bash
# Named volume (Docker-managed, persists across container restarts)
docker run -d -v pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data postgres:16

# Bind mount (host directory mapped into container)
docker run -d -v $(pwd)/src:/app/src myapp:dev

# Read-only bind mount
docker run -d -v $(pwd)/config:/etc/app/config:ro myapp

# tmpfs mount (in-memory, not persisted)
docker run -d --tmpfs /tmp:rw,noexec,size=100m myapp

# Using --mount (more explicit syntax)
docker run -d \
  --mount type=volume,source=pgdata,target=/var/lib/postgresql/data \
  --mount type=bind,source=$(pwd)/config,target=/etc/app/config,readonly \
  myapp
```

See [[docker/volumes]] for volume management and backup strategies.

## Environment Variables

Configure containers at runtime with environment variables:

```bash
# Single variable
docker run -d -e DATABASE_URL=postgres://db:5432/myapp myapp

# Multiple variables
docker run -d \
  -e DATABASE_URL=postgres://db:5432/myapp \
  -e REDIS_URL=redis://cache:6379 \
  -e LOG_LEVEL=info \
  -e NODE_ENV=production \
  myapp

# From host environment
export API_KEY=secret123
docker run -d -e API_KEY myapp  # passes host $API_KEY

# From a file
docker run -d --env-file .env myapp
```

### .env file format:
```
DATABASE_URL=postgres://db:5432/myapp
REDIS_URL=redis://cache:6379
LOG_LEVEL=info
# Comments are supported
NODE_ENV=production
```

## Network Configuration

```bash
# Connect to a user-defined network
docker run -d --network app-network --name api myapp

# Host networking (no isolation, container uses host network directly)
docker run -d --network host myapp

# No networking
docker run -d --network none myapp

# Set hostname and DNS
docker run -d --hostname api.local --dns 8.8.8.8 myapp

# Add hosts file entry
docker run -d --add-host db:192.168.1.100 myapp
```

See [[docker/networking]] for network creation and multi-container communication.

## Resource Limits

```bash
# Memory limits
docker run -d --memory 512m --memory-swap 1g myapp

# CPU limits
docker run -d --cpus 1.5 myapp
docker run -d --cpu-shares 512 myapp  # relative weight

# Process limit
docker run -d --pids-limit 100 myapp

# Combined production constraints
docker run -d \
  --name api \
  --memory 1g \
  --memory-reservation 512m \
  --cpus 2 \
  --pids-limit 200 \
  --restart unless-stopped \
  myapp:v1.0.0
```

## Security Options

```bash
# Run as non-root user
docker run -d --user 1000:1000 myapp
docker run -d --user node myapp

# Read-only filesystem
docker run -d --read-only --tmpfs /tmp myapp

# Drop all capabilities, add only what is needed
docker run -d --cap-drop ALL --cap-add NET_BIND_SERVICE myapp

# Security profile
docker run -d --security-opt no-new-privileges myapp

# Combined secure configuration
docker run -d \
  --name api \
  --user 1000:1000 \
  --read-only \
  --tmpfs /tmp:rw,noexec,size=50m \
  --cap-drop ALL \
  --cap-add NET_BIND_SERVICE \
  --security-opt no-new-privileges \
  myapp:v1.0.0
```

## Override Entrypoint and Command

```bash
# Override CMD (appended after ENTRYPOINT)
docker run myapp node script.js

# Override ENTRYPOINT
docker run --entrypoint /bin/sh myapp -c 'echo hello'

# Override both
docker run --entrypoint python myapp manage.py migrate

# Override working directory
docker run -w /opt/app myapp npm test
```

## Common Patterns

```bash
# Development container with live reload
docker run -d \
  --name dev \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v $(pwd):/app \
  -v /app/node_modules \
  -e NODE_ENV=development \
  myapp:dev \
  npm run dev

# One-off task runner
docker run --rm \
  -v $(pwd):/workspace \
  -w /workspace \
  node:20-alpine \
  npm run migrate

# Database with persistence
docker run -d \
  --name postgres \
  --network app-network \
  -v pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data \
  -e POSTGRES_DB=myapp \
  -e POSTGRES_USER=app \
  -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD_FILE=/run/secrets/db_password \
  --restart unless-stopped \
  postgres:16-alpine
```

sections.title: Basic docker run usage
sections.body: docker run creates and starts a container from an image. Use -d for detached (background) mode. Use --name for human-readable identification. Use --rm for ephemeral containers removed on exit. Use -it for interactive terminal access. The command after the image name overrides the Dockerfile CMD.

sections.title: Port mapping
sections.body: Map container ports to the host with -p host:container. Bind to specific interfaces with 127.0.0.1:host:container for localhost-only access. Use -P to map all EXPOSE'd ports to random host ports. Support TCP (default) and UDP (/udp). Check assigned ports with docker port.

sections.title: Volume mounts
sections.body: Named volumes (-v name:/path) persist data across container restarts and are managed by Docker. Bind mounts (-v /host/path:/container/path) share host directories. Read-only mounts use the :ro suffix. tmpfs mounts store data in memory. The --mount flag provides a more explicit syntax. Use volumes for databases and bind mounts for development.

sections.title: Environment variables
sections.body: Pass environment variables with -e KEY=VALUE. Pass multiple with repeated -e flags. Pass from host environment with -e KEY (no value). Load from file with --env-file .env. Environment files support comments and blank lines. Never pass secrets in plain -e flags in CI -- use Docker secrets or env files excluded from version control.

sections.title: Security options
sections.body: Run as non-root with --user UID:GID. Make filesystem read-only with --read-only (use --tmpfs for writable temp directories). Drop capabilities with --cap-drop ALL and add back only what is needed. Prevent privilege escalation with --security-opt no-new-privileges. These options reduce the blast radius of container compromise.

After running, use logs to view output

scripts.name: Run Container
scripts.description: Run a Docker container with configurable port mapping, volumes, environment, and resource limits
scripts.inline:
docker run -d \
  --name "${CONTAINER_NAME}" \
  ${PORTS:+$(echo "$PORTS" | tr ',' '\n' | sed 's/^/-p /')} \
  ${VOLUMES:+$(echo "$VOLUMES" | tr ',' '\n' | sed 's/^/-v /')} \
  ${ENV_VARS:+$(echo "$ENV_VARS" | tr ',' '\n' | sed 's/^/-e /')} \
  ${ENV_FILE:+--env-file "$ENV_FILE"} \
  ${NETWORK:+--network "$NETWORK"} \
  ${MEMORY:+--memory "$MEMORY"} \
  ${CPUS:+--cpus "$CPUS"} \
  ${RESTART:+--restart "$RESTART"} \
  ${USER_ID:+--user "$USER_ID"} \
  "$IMAGE" ${COMMAND:-} && echo '' && echo "Container '${CONTAINER_NAME}' started" && docker ps --filter "name=${CONTAINER_NAME}" --format 'table {{.ID}}\t{{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Ports}}'

RESTART.description: Restart policy: no, on-failure, always, unless-stopped

scripts.name: Run Interactive Container
scripts.description: Start an interactive container with terminal access for debugging or one-off tasks

scripts.name: Run Development Container
scripts.description: Start a development container with source code mounted for live reload
scripts.inline:
docker run -d \
  --name "${CONTAINER_NAME:-dev}" \
  -p "${HOST_PORT:-3000}:${CONTAINER_PORT:-3000}" \
  -v "${SOURCE_DIR:-.}:/app" \
  -v /app/node_modules \
  -e NODE_ENV=development \
  ${NETWORK:+--network "$NETWORK"} \
  ${ENV_FILE:+--env-file "$ENV_FILE"} \
  "$IMAGE" ${DEV_COMMAND:-npm run dev} && echo '' && echo "Development container '${CONTAINER_NAME:-dev}' started on port ${HOST_PORT:-3000}" && echo "Source mounted from ${SOURCE_DIR:-.}"

SOURCE_DIR.description: Host source directory to mount (default: current directory)

networkIp.description: Container IP address on the network

depends.reason: Docker Engine must be installed and running

author: dojo-community
